1: Are not Are not his days also like the days of an hireling?
2: And as an hireling looketh for the reward of the reward of his work:
3: And wearisome nights are appointed to me.
4: And I am full of tossings to and fro unto the dawning of the day.
5: My skin is broken, and become loathsome.
7: Mine eye shall no more see good.
8: Thine eyes are are upon me, and I am am not.
9: So he that goeth down to the grave shall come up no more more .
10: Neither shall his place know him any more.
11: I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
12: That thou settest a watch over me?
13: My couch shall ease my complaint;
15: And And death rather than my life.
16: Let me alone; for my days are are vanity.
17: And that thou shouldest set thine heart upon him?
18: And And try him every moment?
19: Nor let me alone till I swallow down my spittle?
20: So that I am a burden to myself?
